frameworks.name: fast-scan cyclic voltammetry (FSCV)
frameworks.one_line: electrochemical method exploiting redox chemistry for subsecond neurotransmitter detection

frameworks.name: hybrid CMOS-graphene sensor integration
frameworks.one_line: monolithic integration of graphene sensing electrodes above CMOS readout channels

core_question: Can a hybrid CMOS-graphene array enable scalable subsecond in-vivo dopamine recording with adequate sensitivity?
key_assumptions: planar multilayer graphene is biologically compatible as sensing surface
background FSCV current can be subtracted by a programmable constant current at redox potentials
dual-slope integrating ADC suffices once background is removed
manipulations: scan rate varied across three settings
region-of-interest background-subtraction current programmed at redox potentials
ADC dynamic range constrained after background subtraction
key_findings: four-channel 65-nm CMOS prototype with graphene electrodes fabricated
ROI background subtraction enhanced sensitivity at known dopamine concentration in vitro
dual-slope ADC at 5-10 kHz reached ~60 pA effective resolution
theoretical dopamine detection limit ~6 nM achieved
interpretations: background-subtraction relaxes ADC dynamic range without losing measurement accuracy
platform is suitable for next-generation large-scale in-vivo FSCV dopamine recording
